real_architecture_summary: Runtime event identity comes from spec/events/event_registry_unified.yaml compiled through project/domain/registry_loader.py and surfaced in project/events/event_specs.py. Detector registration also pulls from dynamic family loaders such as project/events/families/interaction.py. State materialization is hardcoded in project/specs/ontology.py and only audited afterward.
top_invariants: All executable events must resolve through the unified registry and ontology mapping.
Alias and canonical event ids must normalize before deconfliction.
Generated ontology coverage artifacts are only trustworthy if they read the same source of truth as runtime.

findings.title: Alias event ids bypass canonicalization in ontology deconfliction
findings.evidence: attach_canonical_event_bundle() maps event_type directly into the bundle map and never calls resolve_event_alias(), so alias ids like VOL_REGIME_SHIFT or BASIS_DISLOCATION do not collapse with canonical ids.
findings.why_it_matters: Equivalent events can survive as separate canonical episodes, corrupting regime routing and episode aggregation.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Pass alias/canonical pairs through attach_canonical_event_bundle() and deconflict_event_episodes() and compare the output bundles.
findings.suggested_remediation: Normalize through resolve_event_alias() before bundle lookup and add alias/canonical regression tests.

findings.title: ontology_audit.json is false-green: it reports no failures while every state source event is missing relative to the checked registry
findings.evidence: ontology_consistency_audit compares state source events to spec/events/canonical_event_registry.yaml, which is only a five-entry legacy stub. The audit emits 70 missing source events but leaves failures empty and --check still passes.
findings.why_it_matters: Operators can see a passing ontology audit when the same artifact proves state-to-event linkage is invalid against its claimed canonical source.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Run project.scripts.ontology_consistency_audit --check and inspect failures plus states_with_missing_source_event.
findings.suggested_remediation: Validate against the unified/compiled registry instead, or escalate non-empty states_with_missing_source_event into failures.

findings.title: Dead interaction-family specs are auto-registered as detectors outside the active ontology contract
findings.evidence: interaction.py registers every ontology event whose id starts with INT_, even though those ids are absent from the active unified registry and ontology mapping. detector_coverage artifacts already report that drift.
findings.why_it_matters: The detector registry exposes callable ids outside the authoritative ontology, creating inconsistent runtime universes depending on which registry surface code consults.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Compare list_registered_event_types() with EVENT_REGISTRY_SPECS and the unified registry, paying attention to INT_* ids and CROSS_ASSET_INTERACTION.
findings.suggested_remediation: Gate interaction registration on compiled-registry membership or promote the intended interaction ids into the authoritative registry/mapping.

findings.title: Generated ontology audits disagree on active-event count because one still relies on top-level per-event YAML presence
findings.evidence: ontology_audit.json reports 69 active specs while event_ontology_audit.json and the compiled registry report 70, because ontology_consistency_audit only scans top-level spec/events/*.yaml and misses active events represented only in the unified registry.
findings.why_it_matters: Repo inventory artifacts disagree about the live event universe, breaking audit comparability and governance trust.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Compare both generated audits with len(get_domain_registry().event_ids) and inspect the missing top-level YAML for CROSS_ASSET_DESYNC_EVENT.
findings.suggested_remediation: Derive both audits from the same unified runtime source or require a per-event YAML for every active event and fail when one is missing.

test_coverage_assessment: Happy-path ontology tests are decent, but alias canonicalization, false-green ontology audit behavior, and exclusion of untracked INT_* detectors are not covered.
docs_specs_code_contradictions: spec/events/canonical_event_registry.yaml is treated as canonical by one audit, but runtime uses a 70-event unified registry.
docs/generated/ontology_audit.json and docs/generated/event_ontology_audit.json disagree on the event inventory.
suggested_regression_tests: A failing ontology-audit test when states_with_missing_source_event is non-empty.
A detector-registry contract test limiting allowed out-of-registry ids.
open_questions_or_unknowns: Whether spec/events/canonical_event_registry.yaml is meant to remain a live audit source or only a legacy file.
